content: It’s natural to be skeptical about how large language models generate text, so here’s a straightforward explanation. These models don’t “understand” language like humans do. Instead, they work by recognizing patterns in huge amounts of text they’ve been trained on. Imagine you’re playing a game where you guess the next word in a sentence based on all the sentences you’ve read before. The model does something similar but on a much larger scale. When given a prompt, it predicts the most likely next word, then the next one after that, building sentences one word at a time. This process relies entirely on statistical patterns, not on comprehension or original thought. So, while these models can produce impressively coherent text, it’s important to remember they’re advanced pattern-matchers, not thinking or reasoning beings. This understanding helps cut through the hype and see what they really do.
